steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must logically complete the argument set up by “suggesting that ______”. The structure is: (1) many studies found a positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury; (2) Guédron found no association in wastewater-disturbed waters, prompting a hypothesis that the association only exists in undisturbed waters; (3) Tjerngren found the association does exist in urban-development-disturbed waters — matching the original studies. The blank must capture what Tjerngren's result implies about the earlier hypothesis.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'the effects of wastewater on the association...should not be taken as indicative of the effects of every type of human disturbance.': Tjerngren's result shows that urban disturbance preserves the association while wastewater breaks it — so wastewater is not representative of all human disturbances. This follows directly. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Guédron...used different methods to measure mercury than Tjerngren...did.': No methodological difference is mentioned in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — 'disturbances linked to wastewater affect significantly more bodies of fresh water than disturbances linked to urban development do.': The passage says nothing about the relative scale of each type of disturbance. ✗
  • Choice D — 'levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury are both much higher in bodies of fresh water impacted by wastewater...': The passage describes whether the association exists, not the absolute levels of either substance. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
